summary.intent: Improve the Greptile code review confidence score from 4/5 to 5/5 by proactively finding and fixing remaining issues in the webhooks crate before the next review cycle.
summary.outcome: Fixed 5 concrete issues across 4 files (auth.rs, normalize.rs, store.rs, page-webhooks.js), added a new migration for a UNIQUE constraint, and pushed all changes. Test count increased from 59 to 62.

repo: Webhooks crate lives at crates/webhooks/ with auth, normalize, and store modules
Frontend webhook config is in crates/web/src/assets/js/page-webhooks.js
Greptile reviews post as inline comments, not summary reviews — review bodies appear empty via GitHub API

code.finding: Stripe signature verification was not handling non-UTF-8 body bytes correctly
UI was missing auth modes and the auth config builder did not handle all HMAC-based modes
HMAC signatures were being logged in headers_json — a security leak that needed redaction
prune_deliveries_before did not cascade deletes to response_actions, leaving orphaned rows
New migration adds UNIQUE constraint on delivery_key to fix TOCTOU race in deduplication logic

workflow: Greptile confidence score is driven by cumulative P1 issue count across review rounds — resolving threads alone may not bump the score without also fixing root patterns
Proactive self-audit before requesting a re-review is more effective than waiting for another Greptile pass
GitHub API returns empty bodies for Greptile reviews because they use inline comments rather than top-level review summaries

friction: Greptile review bodies were empty via GitHub API, making it impossible to directly read the confidence score rationale — had to infer from resolved thread history
Linter had already auto-committed changes before the session picked up, causing a brief confusion about working tree state
open_items: Verify Greptile re-reviews the PR and scores it 5/5 after the new push
The UNIQUE constraint migration (20260407100000_unique_delivery_key.sql) needs to be tested against existing data in staging to ensure no constraint violations on deploy
